person.name: Doug Finke
person.positioning: Doug Finke builds practical AI tools, agent workflows, automation systems, and developer utilities.
person.summary: His work focuses on turning fast-moving AI capabilities into usable tools, experiments, and workflows.

projects.name: ImportExcel
projects.shortDescription: Import and export Excel spreadsheets from PowerShell without requiring Excel.
projects.longDescription: ImportExcel automates spreadsheet creation, reading, reporting, tables, charts, and pivot tables from PowerShell across Windows, Linux, and macOS.
intendedAudience: developers who need scriptable spreadsheet workflows
projects.whyItMatters: It makes repeatable spreadsheet automation available from scripts, terminals, scheduled jobs, and cross-platform automation without desktop Excel.

projects.name: PSAI
projects.shortDescription: PowerShell-native AI agents and multi-agent orchestration.
projects.longDescription: PSAI is a framework for building high-agency AI tools and autonomous agents inside PowerShell workflows, including tool use, orchestration, and provider integration.
proofPoints: Provides agent and tool orchestration patterns
Designed for terminal and script-based AI workflows
projects.whyItMatters: It brings agentic AI patterns into the shell and automation environment where many real operational workflows already happen.

projects.name: PSAISuite
projects.shortDescription: A provider-agnostic PowerShell interface for 15+ GenAI providers.
projects.longDescription: PSAISuite standardizes access to multiple LLM providers from PowerShell, supporting provider switching, benchmarking, tool-calling workflows, and high-performance AI integration.
proofPoints: Includes benchmarking and provider comparison workflows
projects.whyItMatters: It reduces provider lock-in and makes it easier to compare, swap, and operationalize LLMs from real automation surfaces.

projects.name: PSClaudeCode
projects.shortDescription: A PowerShell implementation of a Claude Code-style agent loop.
projects.longDescription: PSClaudeCode explores how a Claude-powered coding agent can work from PowerShell, including iterative task execution, tool use, file operations, permission checks, and conversational workflows.
proofPoints: Implements agent loops, tools, permissions, and file operations
Demonstrates AI agents creating reports through iterative tool use
projects.whyItMatters: It makes the mechanics of coding agents visible and hackable from a familiar command-line environment.

projects.name: PSUnplugged
projects.shortDescription: A PowerShell orchestration shell for the OpenAI Codex App Server.
projects.longDescription: PSUnplugged gives PowerShell users a terminal-native way to drive the Codex runtime while inheriting conversation history, approvals, streamed agent events, MCP servers, skills, and plugins.
proofPoints: Connects PowerShell to the Codex App Server
Supports conversation history and streamed agent events
Positions PowerShell as a control surface for agent workflows
projects.whyItMatters: It treats the shell as a first-class surface for AI-assisted software work rather than forcing every agent workflow into an IDE.
